fable_37_prospects.name: Radisson Blu Hotel Waterfront
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Facade hangs over the breakwater—rope access needs marine permits and boats
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your facade over the water means traditional cleaning needs marine logistics. Drones reach it without permits, boats, or operational disruption.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via radissonhotels.com.

fable_37_prospects.name: Cape Grace Hotel
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Sits directly on the marina—no ground or water access for scaffold or rope teams
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: The marina-side facade can't be scaffolded and rope access needs boats. Drones clean it from standoff distance with zero guest disruption.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via capegrace.com official contact page. Luxury hotel: pitch zero-disruption and image.

fable_37_prospects.name: Atlantic Marina (V&A Marina Residential)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Apartment facades directly over water—scaffold impossible, rope access needs marine logistics
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your apartment facades rise straight out of the marina. Residents currently live behind salt-hazed glass because nobody can reach it. Drones can, without boats or permits.
fable_37_prospects.notes: VERIFIED CONTACT: property management 021 418 0128, ops 079 392 3019. One of the few with a confirmed phone—call first.

fable_37_prospects.name: V&A Waterfront Management Co
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: 123 hectares, 50+ buildings, heritage constraints on many
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: One contract here = work across Clock Tower, Victoria Wharf skylights, Silo District, Marina facades. Your heritage buildings especially can't be scaffolded.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Call switchboard 021 408 7600, ask for Facilities Director, or use the supplier enquiry form at waterfront.co.za. IMPORTANT: active heliport on precinct—lead with SACAA ROC/operator approvals and insurance in the very first contact or you're dead on arrival.

fable_37_prospects.name: Zeitz MOCAA / The Silo Hotel
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Pillowed, faceted glass windows—no gondola track can follow the geometry; the single best drone-cleaning case in Cape Town
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: The Silo's bulging faceted glass is untrackable by any BMU or gondola. It is the definitive drone-cleaning building: we follow the geometry, nothing else can.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Approach via V&A Waterfront Management (see that prospect) and The Royal Portfolio. Win this one and it's the best case study in the country.

fable_37_prospects.name: CTICC (Cape Town International Convention Centre)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Massive angled glass frontage plus inaccessible roof spans—no BMU coverage
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your angled glass frontage and wide roof spans can't be walked or tracked by a gondola. Drones cover both without closing a single event day.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via cticc.co.za. Expect a vendor/procurement process—have the SACAA file ready.

fable_37_prospects.name: GrandWest Casino
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Huge themed roofscape and skylights—no safe walk access, closure for scaffold is unthinkable
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your themed roofscape and skylights can't be walked, and the property never closes. Drones clean over live operations with zero downtime.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Owner: Sun International—land this and pitch the wider Sun portfolio.

fable_37_prospects.name: Canal Walk Shopping Centre
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Hectares of skylights over a trading mall—closing the mall for scaffolding is unthinkable
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your skylights sit over live retail. Scaffold means closed trading floors; drones mean clean skylights with the mall open the whole time.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Centre management via canalwalk.co.za.

fable_37_prospects.name: Cape Town Stadium (DHL Stadium)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Glass/polycarbonate roof famously grimes over and nobody can walk it
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: The stadium roof is known for griming over and there is no safe walk access. Drone cleaning is the only realistic maintenance path.
fable_37_prospects.notes: City-owned: requires City of Cape Town vendor registration FIRST. Start the registration before pitching.

fable_37_prospects.name: Old Mutual Building (Mutual Heights)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Art deco granite facade—anchor points and scaffold fixings prohibited or damaging
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: The art deco granite facade can't be drilled and scaffold fixings would damage it. Drones clean heritage stone with zero contact.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Mixed residential/commercial—approach via building management / body corporate.

fable_37_prospects.name: Iziko Museums (SA Museum, Slave Lodge)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: National heritage buildings—no drilling, no scaffold fixings, image-sensitive
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your heritage facades legally can't take anchors or scaffold fixings. Drone cleaning is contact-free and preserves the fabric of the buildings.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via iziko.org.za. State-adjacent: expect procurement forms.

fable_37_prospects.name: UCT Properties & Services
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Ivy-and-sandstone heritage buildings across upper campus—scaffold-averse, many buildings
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Upper campus sandstone can't be drilled and scaffolding a working university is disruptive. One relationship covers dozens of campus buildings.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact the Properties & Services department via uct.ac.za. Effectively a multiplier: one dept, whole campus.

fable_37_prospects.name: Groote Kerk & historic city bowl churches
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Spires and clerestory glass—unreachable without towers of scaffold on heritage fabric
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Spires and high clerestory glass have likely never been properly cleaned—scaffold costs make it impossible. Drones reach them at a fraction of the price.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Each church has its own council/office. Low value individually but easy wins and visible portfolio pieces.

fable_37_prospects.name: Wine Estates: Vergelegen, Spier, Babylonstoren, Groot Constantia
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Heritage manor gables, large winery roofs, growing rooftop PV—image-sensitive, scaffolding a non-starter in tourist season
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your heritage gables can't be scaffolded during tourist season and your winery roofs and PV are losing efficiency to dust. Drones handle all three invisibly, off-season or on.
fable_37_prospects.notes: 4 separate targets—contact each estate/events office via their websites: vergelegen.co.za, spier.co.za, babylonstoren.com, grootconstantia.co.za. Work them one at a time.

fable_37_prospects.name: Spear REIT
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Western Cape-only landlord: 29 properties, PV installed at 16 of 30 assets generating 5.2MW+, scaling to 7.2MW
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: You have over 5.2MW of rooftop PV across 16 assets, scaling to 7.2MW. Dirty panels lose 15-25% output—at your scale that's serious money. We maintain arrays at 90%+ output, no one walking your roofs.
fable_37_prospects.notes: THE #1 COLD CALL on this list. Western Cape-only landlord = every asset is reachable. Contact via spearprop.co.za; HQ is 16th Floor, 2 Long St. One contract = 29 properties.

fable_37_prospects.name: Black River Park
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: One of SA's largest rooftop PV installations spread across a multi-building office park
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: You run one of the largest rooftop PV installations in the country. Every percent of soiling is measurable lost generation—we keep the whole park's array at peak output without roof crews.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Park management via blackriverpark.co.za. Note: Redefine Properties holds an interest—see that prospect too.

fable_37_prospects.name: Infini Solar
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Commercial/industrial solar installer with a national installed base needing O&M cleaning
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your installed base needs recurring panel cleaning you don't want to staff. We become your subcontracted cleaning arm: you sell the O&M contract, we fly it.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Pitch as their subcontracted cleaning arm, not a client. B2B partnership angle.

fable_37_prospects.name: SOLA Group
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: IPP with commercial PV portfolios needing ongoing O&M cleaning
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: As an IPP your revenue is directly tied to generation. Soiling losses of 15-25% are recoverable—we deliver drone-based cleaning across your commercial PV portfolio as a recurring O&M line.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via solagroup.com. IPP = generation is revenue; quantify the pitch in kWh.

fable_37_prospects.name: Ingenuity Property Investments
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Cape Town-focused fund with a 118,987m² portfolio incl. 117 On Strand and 20 Vineyard Rd
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your CT-focused portfolio (117 On Strand, 20 Vineyard Rd and more) includes facades no rope team can economically service. One relationship covers nearly 119,000m² of assets.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via ingenuityproperty.com/contact-us. Office: Intaba, 25 Protea Rd, Claremont.

fable_37_prospects.name: Broll Property Group (Cape Town)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Manages dozens of commercial buildings in CT—approved-vendor list is the door
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: You manage dozens of commercial buildings, many with no BMU or davit systems. One approved-vendor listing gives your PMs a drone option on every hard-access facade.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Goal: get on their approved-vendor list via broll.com. Vendor onboarding first, jobs follow.

fable_37_prospects.name: Excellerate JHI (Cape Town)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Major property manager—same approved-vendor play as Broll
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your managed portfolio includes buildings where scaffold quotes kill every cleaning proposal. As an approved vendor we give your PMs a priced alternative that actually gets signed off.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via excellerate.co.za. Same play as Broll: vendor list first.

fable_37_prospects.name: Trafalgar Property Management (Cape Town)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Body corporates for Atlantic seaboard high-rises—most have no BMU/davit, so window cleaning needs full scaffold quotes trustees always reject
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your Atlantic seaboard buildings are visibly dirty because every scaffold quote gets rejected by trustees. We quote a fraction of scaffold cost—trustees finally have a number they can approve.
fable_37_prospects.notes: FASTEST CASH on the list: trustees decide quickly, buildings are visibly filthy, zero procurement bureaucracy. Contact via trafalgar.co.za. Anchor price against the R150k+ scaffold quote, never against rope-access rates.

fable_37_prospects.name: One&Only Cape Town
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Island rooms and facades over the marina canal—no scaffold ground, rope needs boats
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your island rooms sit over the canal—no scaffold can reach them and rope teams need marine logistics. Drones clean them from the air with zero guest disruption.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via oneandonlyresorts.com. Ultra-luxury: zero-disruption pitch.

fable_37_prospects.name: The President Hotel
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Facade drops toward rocks and sea—no ground for scaffold at all
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your seaward facade drops straight to the rocks—there is literally no ground to scaffold from. Salt haze builds until someone can reach it. We can.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via presidenthotel.co.za.

fable_37_prospects.name: Rabie Property Group (Century City canal-side)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Offices and apartments rising straight out of the canals—Rabie manages the whole precinct
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your canal-side buildings rise straight out of the water—unreachable by scaffold or rope without marine setups. One relationship with Rabie covers the entire Century City node.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via rabie.co.za. One relationship = the whole Century City precinct.

fable_37_prospects.name: Netcare Christiaan Barnard Memorial Hospital
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Sheer glass tower and hospitals can't have workers dangling past ICU windows
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: A hospital can't have rope workers dangling past ICU windows. Drones clean your glass tower from standoff distance—no faces at the window, no disruption to care.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via netcare.co.za. Win here = pitch the national Netcare group.

fable_37_prospects.name: The Westin Cape Town
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Full-height glass tower at the CTICC precinct
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Full-height glass with limited BMU coverage means sections of your tower stay hazy between rare deep cleans. Drones cover the whole envelope on a regular cycle.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via marriott.com / hotel direct. Neighbours the CTICC—bundle the pitch.

fable_37_prospects.name: Mediclinic (Head Office + Panorama Hospital)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Hospital glass—same no-disruption logic as Netcare: no workers past patient windows
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Patients can't have rope workers outside their windows. Drone cleaning keeps your glass clean with standoff distance and zero clinical disruption.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via mediclinic.co.za. Two targets: head office + Panorama hospital.

fable_37_prospects.name: Bishops Diocesan College
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Heritage stone buildings and chapels—scaffold fixings damage the fabric, image-driven institution
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your heritage stone and chapel glass can't take scaffold fixings, and scaffolding during term looks terrible. Drones clean contact-free, holidays or term time.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact estate manager via bishops.org.za.

fable_37_prospects.name: SACS / Rondebosch Boys / Herschel
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Heritage school estates—same scaffold-averse, image-driven logic as Bishops; three more doors
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Heritage school buildings can't be scaffolded without damage and disruption. Drone cleaning is invisible to parents and gentle on hundred-year-old stone.
fable_37_prospects.notes: 3 separate doors—same estate-manager pitch as Bishops. Work them after a Bishops win for the referral effect.

fable_37_prospects.name: Stellenbosch University Facilities Management
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Historic Victoria Street buildings plus a large PV rollout on campus roofs
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: You have two problems we solve at once: heritage Victoria Street facades that can't be scaffolded, and a growing campus PV fleet losing output to dust. One vendor, both jobs.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact facilities management via sun.ac.za. Dual pitch: heritage + solar.

fable_37_prospects.name: Artscape Theatre Centre
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Vast brutalist facades and roof with no access infrastructure
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your brutalist facades and wide roof have no built-in access at all—decades of grime prove it. Drones are the first realistic way to clean the building.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via artscape.co.za. State-adjacent: expect vendor forms.

fable_37_prospects.name: Shoprite Checkers Distribution Centres
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Enormous PV-covered distribution centre roofs—walking crews on that scale is slow and risky
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your DC roofs carry PV at massive scale. Soiling losses across that area are enormous and measurable—drone cleaning recovers output without crews walking your roofs.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Group facilities via shopriteholdings.co.za. Corporate procurement: patience + paperwork.

fable_37_prospects.name: Growthpoint Properties (Western Cape)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Co-owner of the V&A plus large CT office/industrial assets with rooftop PV
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your WC portfolio spans the V&A co-ownership, offices and industrial assets with rooftop PV. One vendor listing covers hard-access facades and solar maintenance across all of it.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via growthpoint.co.za. National landlord #1 to register with.

fable_37_prospects.name: Redefine Properties (Western Cape)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: National landlord with WC assets incl. an interest in Black River Park
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Your WC assets include Black River Park's PV interest and hard-access commercial facades. One approved-vendor relationship covers both service lines.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Contact via redefine.co.za. Second national landlord to register with (after Growthpoint).

fable_37_prospects.name: Outdoor Media: Tractor Outdoor (+ Primedia, JCDecaux)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: High-site billboards currently need cranes or riggers to wash—a grimy billboard is unsold inventory
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: A grimy billboard is unsold inventory. Washing high sites today means cranes and riggers; we do it by drone at a fraction of the cost, keeping your faces sellable.
fable_37_prospects.notes: START WITH TRACTOR: Cape Town HQ'd, tractoroutdoor.com. Then Primedia Outdoor and JCDecaux with the case study. Nobody else is pitching this.

fable_37_prospects.name: Commercial Greenhouses (Philippi / Stellenbosch)
fable_37_prospects.access_moat: Dirty greenhouse glass directly cuts light transmission and yield—no one services this market
fable_37_prospects.why_drones_matter: Dirty glass cuts light transmission, and light is yield. You can quantify our value in crop terms per season—and right now nobody else offers this service at all.
fable_37_prospects.notes: Unserved market. Pitch in yield/crop terms—growers can calculate the ROI themselves. Drive Philippi and pick visibly dirty houses first.
